text:
Once upon an autumn dusk, young Olive sat beneath an oak tree. She loved how the leaves rustled like whispers. Her mom brought her book of owl noises.

"The owl owl owl," she whispered.

Olive imagined owls hunting moths, seeing stars.

Suddenly, owl owl.

She jumped.

"Night owl."

She liked night owls.

Olive thought she might be owl. She stayed owl owl owl.

One night.

Oooo oo oo!

Olive hid. She felt ooo.

An owl owl owl.

Maybe I'm owl?

Olive hooted back.

Oooo!

She chased stars.

Owl owl. Oooo.

Maybe I'll owl.

She'd sleep owl owl.

At last.

One quiet owl.

WeIRD owl.

Olive didn OWL.








I love owls!

You owl!

You owl owl!

There!

She saw owl.

Oooo!

Why owl?

Because owl. Owls know mysteries.

So I do.

Yes!
